perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. However, disclosure by the Disclosing Party of Confidential Information does not constitute a warranty that the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, or adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Transferring Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. However, disclosure by the Disclosing Party of Confidential Information does not constitute a warranty that the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, or adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Transferring Party' in the first sentence creates confusion. The second sentence still refers to 'Disclosing Party,' making it unclear which party's actions are being discussed. The sentence in the second part, refers to a party which is not defined anymore in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Transferring Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. However, disclosure by the Disclosing Party of Confidential Information does not constitute a warranty that the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, or adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Disclosing Party' to 'Transferring Party' in the first sentence of the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the second sentence, which still refers to 'Disclosing Party.' This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, so it suffers from the same contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Receiving Party becomes aware that Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party is inadvertently or accidentally disclosed by it, the Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in writing within five (5) working days of the discovery of such disclosure, and shall take all commercially reasonable precautions to avoid further dissemination of the Confidential Information that has been disclosed, as well as take any and all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent disclosure of any additional Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Recipient becomes aware that Protected Data of a Discloser is inadvertently or accidentally disclosed by it, the Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in writing within five (5) working days of the discovery of such disclosure, and shall take all commercially reasonable precautions to avoid further dissemination of the Confidential Information that has been disclosed, as well as take any and all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent disclosure of any additional Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient', 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data', and 'Disclosing Party' to 'Discloser' in the first part creates confusion. The rest of the paragraph still uses the original terms, so now it is not clear which term is been refferred, and now creates ambiguity. The rest of the text refers to 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party' and 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Recipient becomes aware that Protected Data of a Discloser is inadvertently or accidentally disclosed by it, the Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in writing within five (5) working days of the discovery of such disclosure, and shall take all commercially reasonable precautions to avoid further dissemination of the Confidential Information that has been disclosed, as well as take any and all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent disclosure of any additional Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient', 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data', and 'Disclosing Party' to 'Discloser'. However, the rest of the paragraph, as well as the 'Contradicted Text', continues to use the original terms ('Receiving Party', 'Disclosing Party', 'Confidential Information'). This creates a direct contradiction because it is unclear which terms are being referred to in which context, leading to ambiguity and potentially conflicting obligations, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
